_comment: Class D (recursion-guard). A protocol slot / helper that recurses into a contained Python object without a recursion guard overflows the NATIVE stack on a deep/cyclic object → SIGSEGV (not a catchable RecursionError). RustPython guards frame depth with `with_recursion` and per-object repr with `ReprGuard`. CALIBRATION (v0.2.1): `.repr(vm)` and `.rich_compare(` are DISPATCH-guarded one level up — `PyObject::repr` and `PyObject::_cmp` (protocol/object.rs) both wrap `with_recursion`, so a slot recursing via them cannot overflow. Only `.hash(vm)` (PyObject::hash calls the slot directly, unguarded), `.str(vm)`, `__reduce__`, and the getset/helper parameter walk (`make_parameters`/`subs_parameters`, the RUSTPY-0007a / CPython #154275 class) are the real gap.
